markdown.markdown:
This pipeline is a short-read functional placement workflow for metatranscriptome (metaT) ORFs. It assigns taxonomy by placing gene fragments onto fixed, curated reference gene trees. Instead of relying on sequence similarity alone.

-   **Who is this for?** 
Researchers working with metaT short-read ORF (contig) datasets who want: phylogenetically informed taxonomic assignments

-   **Where can I run it?** 
On a remote HPC server (e.g., HiPerGator) or local system (i.e., your computer; provided the required software is installed) 

-   **What's the end goal?** 
For each gene × metaT sample, produce a .jplace file containing phylogenetic placements. Each transcript is assigned taxonomy based on its position on the reference tree, which acts as a tally of gene-specific transcript placements.

---

This tutorial walks through a series of shell (.sh) scripts: Part 1 (A, B, C), Part 2, Part 3. Each corresponds to a specific stage of the pipeline. Brief descriptions are provided below.

::: {.callout-note title="PIPELINE STAGES"}
| Script | Purpose | Main outputs |
|--------|---------|--------------|
| 1_A | Build gene-specific reference package and HMM | `.refpkg/`, `.hmm` |
| 1_B | Submit parallel jobs across samples | SLURM jobs |
| 1_C | Detect contigs, align, and place onto gene tree | `.jplace` |
| 2 | Aggregate placements and export taxonomy tables | `*.taxID.csv` |
| 3 | Classify placements and generate normalized counts | `*.counts.csv` |
:::

.

.

## Required Packages

::: {.callout-note title="REQUIRED PACKAGES"}
| Package    | Version | Notes |
|------------|---------|-------|
| Python     | 2.7     | Build sequence metadata tables |
| taxtastic  | 0.9.1   | Taxonomy handling and refpkg construction |
| seqmagick  | 0.8.4   | FASTA ↔ Stockholm format conversion |
| hmmer      | 3.4     | HMM construction and contig detection |
| pplacer    | 1.1     | Phylogenetic placement |
:::

.

.

## Starting Files

Before running **Part 1_A**!! 

These files are absolutely needed for Part 1_A. And they are are reused throughout all stages of the pipeline.

::: {.callout-info title="Gene list files (drive array jobs)"}
| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `genesAbbr.txt` | Gene abbreviations used in filenames and arrays |
| `genes.txt` | Corresponding gene directory names |
| `*.trimal2.fasta` | Trimmed reference alignment |
| `RAxML_result*.tre` | Reference phylogenetic tree |
| `RAxML_info*.tre` | Tree statistics file |
| `taxonomy.db` | Taxonomy database used by `taxit` |
| `*.sampleList.txt` | List of sample IDs for dataset |
| `*.orfs40.fasta.gz` | ORF/contig FASTA files (one per sample) |
:::

.

.

## Scripts

Let's get started! 

> **Mental reminder:**  
> - **Part 1:** Builds gene-specific references once.  
> - **Part 2:** Aggregates placements across samples.  
> - **Part 3:** Converts placements into normalized count tables.

### Part 1
[1_pplacer_A-C](scripts/part1.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .me-2}

### Part 2
[2_pplacer](scripts/part2.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .me-2}

### Part 3
[3_pplacer](scripts/part3.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .me-2}
